rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES, LLC, AS ASSIGNEE OF CA VS. MICHAEL NADELMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Void The Judgment And For Leave To Defend The Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT MICHAEL NADELMAN's Notice Of Motion To Void The Judgment And For Leave To Defend The Action. The motion is granted because the opposing party failed to provide a courtesy copy of it papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B, despite the court's additional directive to opposing party on 9/1/12 to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC, VS. KILLARNEY CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From, And For Production Of Documents By, Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc., And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC. Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From, And For Production Of Documents By, Santos & Urrutia Associates, Inc., And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required: It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to explain their positions on the relevance to issues in this litigation of documents in the possession of Santoa & Urratia Associates regarding the work they are doing for the new owner, Raintree 973 Market, LLC. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: PAUL H. WERMER et al VS. SALLY A. UNTERBERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enforce Settlement And Appoint A Referee And For Costs And Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs PAUL WERMER, CAROL BROWNSON's Motion To Enforce Settlement And Appoint A Referee And For Costs And Attorneys' Fees. The motion is continued to October 24, 2012. Plaintiff is ordered to provide a copy of the stamped plans as soon as possible per the representation of her counsel Mr. Polito. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith regarding the coordination of the construction. Despite the language of § 7.9 of the Access Agreement, written and oral communication between the parties and contractors may be necessary to effectuate the overarching purpose of the access and settlement agreements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD LA FORCE et al VS. WAYNE CORCORAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ronald La Force'S Motion To Enforce Prior Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2012, Line 5, Plaintiff Ronald La Force'S Motion To Enforce Prior Order Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. The intent of the Court's order entered August 8, 2012 was that Defendant Corcoran pay for the cost of obtaining his OTR Transcript from FINRA. Exhibit 1 to the Declaration of Mitchell J. Albert shows that FINRA has authorized the release of the OTR transcript records. Defendant Corcoran shall within three days pay Veritext for the OTR Transcript records and then immediately upon receiving the records provide a copy to Plaintiffs' counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL GRAGNANI VS. CLARENDON NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT MEDIATECHNICS CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff has adequately pled facts to allege breach of contract and fraud. On the face of the complaint, Plaintiff is not barred by the Statute of Limitations or Statute of Frauds. Defendant raises issues of fact that are not the subject of a demurrer. The request for judicial notice is not opposed and so is granted, but not for the truth of the statements in those items. E.g., Ragland v. U.S. Bank Natl. Assn., __Cal.App.4th__ (September 11, 2012, No. G 045580). For future filings, the parties are instructed to comply with all applicable California Rules of Court, and in particular rules 2.104, 2.105 and 2.108. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL GRAGNANI VS. CLARENDON NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 21, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT MEDIATECHNICS CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendant Gree International, Inc. shall produce documents responsive to Plaintiff Zombie, Inc.'s Requests for Production of Documents, Set Two, and a privilege log listing documents withheld on a claim of privilege within 21 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
